noveltyJustification: This iteration located the exact Rust residency boundary, identified local batched inference as the only clear new capability, and converted hashing, watching, and caching ideas into measured conditional gates or ruled-out ownership transfers.
focus: Which indexing and embedding automations become materially better with Rust?
answeredQuestions: Which indexing and embedding automations become materially better with Rust?

ruledOut.reason: JavaScript and SQLite already own lifecycle and atomic publication; only parallel precomputation is conditionally material.

ruledOut.approach: Rust filesystem watcher as correctness authority
ruledOut.reason: Filesystem events can be lost or reshaped and must remain hints for canonical rescans.
The existing retrieval-hash and profile cache already provides semantic content-addressed reuse.
The single transaction is a correctness boundary rather than incidental serialization.

label: Local batched inference is the only clear new Rust-resident capability
Parallel verification is gated by measured cold-build residency
Filesystem watching remains a non-authoritative rescan hint
The existing embedding cache already provides semantic content-addressed reuse
JavaScript and SQLite must retain queue and publication ownership
Parallel results must be deterministically ordered before existing generation or parity oracles consume them
Filesystem events cannot replace canonical reconciliation and two-observation missing-item handling
Styles tree is approximately 135 MiB across 7776 files
Vector drain invokes one asynchronous embedder callback per serially processed job

reason: This would transfer orchestration and publication ownership without evidence of material resident compute.

direction: Rust filesystem watcher as correctness authority
reason: Native events are not complete or stable enough to replace canonical scans.
The existing retrieval-hash and profile cache already supplies the required semantic reuse.
The single-writer transaction is an atomicity contract.
